page: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. The server is powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz across 16 physical cores with a single thread per core configuration. This instance includes 320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address, with no dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing the instance in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores align with the average tier (middle 50%). Memory performance is a notable highlight; the PassMark memory score ranks in the top tier (top 10%), supported by strong uncached read and write memory bandwidth (top 25%). Single-threaded bzip3 decompression also reaches the top 10%, while multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and Redis SET operations perform at average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation model provides a resource-dense profile that balances compute power and memory capacity without the premium of dedicated CPU cores. This makes the instance suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and memory-sensitive applications that do not require sustained 100% CPU utilization. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, intermediate database hosting, and general-purpose application servers.
description: Vultr voc-g-16c-64gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance configured with 16 vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, this x86_64 server delivers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside strong uncached memory bandwidth. Multi-core execution, Redis SET operations, and database operations perform within the average range. Lacking a GPU, this instance offers a cost-effective, resource-dense profile suitable for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and moderate database workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring AMD EPYC Genoa processors, high-speed NVMe storage, and top-tier single-core performance.
